vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1233: PAN-1148 follow-up — IssueCard primitive gradient-overlay + merge-ready border tokens

plan.title: PAN-1148 follow-up — IssueCard primitive gradient-overlay + merge-ready border tokens
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: The IssueCard primitive (src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/primitives/IssueCard.tsx) deviates from PRD §4.7.6 in two ways that surface on both Board and Pipeline. (1) PR-1 — the card uses a tinted gradient overlay div (line 86, with the `tone` ternary on lines 46–52) plus a 6px-wide accent strip flush to the card edge, instead of the spec's solid `color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 92%, var(--color-white))` background plus a 2px `::before` priority bar inset 12px top/bottom. (2) PR-2 — merge-ready cards render an amber border (`border-warning/60` on line 79) instead of the PRD-mandated `color-mix(in srgb, var(--success) 32%, transparent)`. This is the same inversion previously flagged at the Board surface (B-3) re-appearing at the primitive layer; fixing it here propagates to every surface that uses the primitive.
narratives.Proposal: Fix both deviations directly in the primitive. (Bead 1) Delete the gradient overlay div and the entire `tone` ternary; apply a single solid background via a new `.issue-card-surface` utility class in src/dashboard/frontend/src/index.css (matching the @layer components pattern already used by `.badge-bg-success` / `.drawer-gate-border-pass`); slim the accent strip from `w-1.5` (6px) to `w-0.5` (2px) while preserving the existing 12px top/bottom inset. (Bead 2) Replace `border-warning/60` on line 79 with the existing `.badge-border-success` utility class (which already resolves to the PRD's success-32%-transparent token). Both fixes are mechanical and independently shippable; verification is an isolated-Playwright UAT pass that confirms a merge-ready card and a default card both render per spec on the kanban board.

autoDecisions.summary: Use a CSS utility class (`.issue-card-surface`) in index.css for the spec background rather than a Tailwind arbitrary-value class (`bg-[color-mix(...)]`).
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the existing codebase pattern (`.badge-bg-success`, `.drawer-gate-border-pass`, `.badge-shimmer-rtm`) for design-token utilities, avoids Tailwind JIT escaping pitfalls with commas/spaces inside `color-mix()`, and keeps the design-token surface centralized in index.css where the rest of the theme lives.

autoDecisions.summary: Reuse the existing `.badge-border-success` class (index.css:214) for the merge-ready border rather than defining a new class.
autoDecisions.rationale: The class already evaluates to exactly the PRD-specified `color-mix(in srgb, var(--success) 32%, transparent)`. Introducing a parallel class would duplicate the token and create future drift risk.

autoDecisions.summary: Delete the `tone` variable and its four branches entirely; do not keep it as a no-op fallback.
autoDecisions.rationale: Per CLAUDE.md (`no bandaids`, `no backwards-compat shims`), unused branches are deleted. State differentiation moves entirely to border + verb badge, as the PRD specifies.

autoDecisions.summary: Accent strip keeps `left-0` (flush to card edge) — only its width and top/bottom inset are constrained by the PRD.
autoDecisions.rationale: PRD §4.7.6 specifies `inset 12px top/bottom` and `2px` width; left-edge inset is not specified. Current `left-0` is preserved; only `w-1.5` → `w-0.5` changes.

autoDecisions.summary: Browser UAT runs in an isolated Playwright instance (its own profile/context), not piggybacking on any existing session.
autoDecisions.rationale: Repository workspace rule (Playwright Isolation section of the planning prompt) requires browser verification to be reproducible inside an isolated session so concurrent agents don't interfere.

items.title: Replace gradient overlay with solid spec background + slim priority bar to 2px

narrative.Action: Edit src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/primitives/IssueCard.tsx and src/dashboard/frontend/src/index.css. In index.css, add a new utility class inside the existing `@layer components` block: `.issue-card-surface { background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 92%, var(--color-white)); }`. In IssueCard.tsx, (a) delete the `tone` variable and all four ternary branches that compute it; (b) delete the JSX line `<div className={cn('pointer-events-none absolute inset-x-0 top-0 h-20 bg-gradient-to-br', tone)} />` (currently line 86); (c) add `issue-card-surface` to the root div's className list, replacing the inherited `bg-card` for the default path (keep `bg-card` semantics for any specific-state branches such as `bg-destructive/10`, `bg-warning/10`, `bg-primary/10` that already exist on lines 77–81 — those overlay states layer on top of the surface and stay); (d) change the accent strip width from `w-1.5` to `w-0.5` on line 87 while keeping `absolute bottom-[12px] left-0 top-[12px] rounded-r-[2px] ${accent}`. After the edits, run `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` from the workspace root and confirm clean.

subItems.title: The JSX element matching `pointer-events-none absolute inset-x-0 top-0 h-20 bg-gradient-to-br` no longer appears anywhere in IssueCard.tsx (grep returns zero matches).

The `tone` local variable and all four of its ternary branches (`from-destructive/12 …`, `from-warning/20 …`, `from-primary/16 …`, `from-surface-overlay/60 …`) are removed from IssueCard.tsx — grep for `from-destructive/12`, `from-warning/20`, `from-primary/16`, `from-surface-overlay/60` inside this file returns zero matches.

A new `.issue-card-surface` utility class exists in src/dashboard/frontend/src/index.css inside an `@layer components` block, with the declaration `background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--background) 92%, var(--color-white));` exactly as written (whitespace tolerant).

The IssueCard root `<div>` applies the `issue-card-surface` class on the default (non-state) path so a card with no special-state props renders against the spec background. State-specific background overlays (`bg-destructive/10` for stuck/unhealthy, `bg-warning/10` for merge-ready, `bg-primary/10` for bulkSelected) remain intact.
The priority accent strip uses width class `w-0.5` (2px) instead of `w-1.5` (6px); top/bottom inset stays at `12px` via the existing `top-[12px] bottom-[12px]` classes; left edge stays at `left-0`.
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` (run from the workspace root) all complete with exit code 0 after the edits.

Browser UAT in an isolated Playwright session: navigate to the dashboard's kanban board on the host (https://pan.localhost) with at least one default issue card and one running issue card visible. Take a screenshot. Confirm visually that (a) no diagonal gradient tint exists at the top of any card, (b) the priority bar on the left edge reads as a thin 2px line inset ~12px top/bottom, and (c) the card background reads as a single uniform tone slightly lighter than the surrounding column (the `color-mix(--background 92%, --color-white)` lift). The Playwright session MUST be launched with its own browser context — do not reuse an existing user-data dir.

items.title: Switch merge-ready border from `border-warning/60` to success-32% token

narrative.Action: Edit src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/primitives/IssueCard.tsx, line 79 (inside the `mergeReadyCard` branch of the className ternary). Replace the literal `border-warning/60 bg-warning/10 shadow-md` with `badge-border-success bg-warning/10 shadow-md`. The `.badge-border-success` class is defined at src/dashboard/frontend/src/index.css:214 and resolves to `border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--success) 32%, transparent)` — the exact value PRD §4.7.6 prescribes for merge-ready cards. Keep `bg-warning/10` and `shadow-md` untouched; only the border-color token changes. Do NOT alter the `selected` ring (`ring-warning/70` on line 75) — that is a different surface (selection focus), not a merge-ready signal, and is out of scope for this issue. Re-run quality gates after the edit.

subItems.title: Line 79 of IssueCard.tsx no longer contains the substring `border-warning/60`. (grep for `border-warning/60` inside IssueCard.tsx returns zero matches.)
The same line applies `badge-border-success` in the merge-ready branch; the `bg-warning/10` and `shadow-md` classes are preserved alongside it.
The `ring-warning/70` class on line 75 (selection ring) is untouched — grep confirms it still appears exactly once in the file.
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` (run from the workspace root) all complete with exit code 0 after the edit.

Browser UAT in an isolated Playwright session: navigate to the dashboard's kanban board (https://pan.localhost) with at least one merge-ready issue card visible. Screenshot the merge-ready card and confirm the border reads as green (success token), not amber. If no merge-ready card is naturally available, surface this as a hazard rather than skipping the check — do not mark this AC done without a green-border screenshot. The Playwright session MUST run in its own isolated browser context.
